title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC made its NWSL debut against Gotham FC, attracting over 30,000 fans at Gillette Stadium.
The match ended with Gotham FC winning 1-0, and tied a league record with eight yellow cards issued.
Legacy FC's aggressive style led to a red card for Bianca St-Georges in the 77th minute of the match.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys celebrated their 30th anniversary with a show on March 13 at MGM Music Hall at Fenway.
The band performed classic hits and new songs, including their latest single 'Citizen I.C.E.' from 'New England Forever.'
Heather Fahey and Austin Sparkman joined Dropkick Murphys for special performances during the St. Patrick's Day kickoff show.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett, from New Braintree, Mass., was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11.
He supports Joslin Diabetes Center, crediting it for providing hope and confidence after his diagnosis.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter receives her fifth Oscar nomination, becoming the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
Carter previously made history as the first Black person to win an Oscar for Best Costume Design in 2019.
She utilized Massachusetts resources for 'Sinners' costumes, including the Brimfield Antique Flea Market for vintage garments.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if a $18.5 million property-tax override doesn't pass.
Without the override, class sizes could increase to 30 students, and programs like music may be cut.
Brookline's financial strain is due to rising costs and limited tax revenue growth under Proposition 2½.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Massachusetts PETS Act aims to ban pet shop sales of dogs and cats to curb reliance on puppy mills.
The proposed legislation would allow pet stores to partner with shelters and rescue groups for pet adoptions.
The act seeks to expand pet access in state-aided housing and prevent breed-based insurance denials.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: State officials urge water conservation as drought intensifies across Massachusetts despite heavy winter snowfall.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts now face Level 3 Critical Drought, with the Connecticut River Valley at Level 2.
Upcoming spring rains may help, but sustained precipitation is needed to restore groundwater and surface water levels.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay explores why some women are obscuring boyfriends on social media, questioning traditional relationship norms.
Joseph's viral piece suggests women may avoid centering men to maintain independence and redefined social identity.
Joseph's insights, discussed on the Love Letters podcast, challenge the assumption that coupling with men is paramount for women.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, 27, of Boston, is running the marathon supporting The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp offers children with serious medical conditions a fun, medically-safe environment free of charge.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, a camp alum, now in remission, serves as a camp counselor.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles are not trading wide receiver A.J. Brown to the Patriots at this time despite interest.
Trade discussions could resume near June 1 for cap benefits, potentially lowering A.J. Brown's trade price.
The Patriots offered a first- and third-round pick for Brown, which Philadelphia reportedly declined.
